blocks.text: kept Marcus in his peripheral on the walk back. He noticed the way Marcus watched people leave — like he was already planning the next reason to bring them together.
filed Wren separately from the others. The ones who say least usually notice most.
held onto it. Nadia had volunteered useful information before anyone thought to ask. He'd only met a few people who knew how to do that naturally.
kept it. They were people around a fire.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: They seem good. All of them. Marcus especially — he's one of those people who makes a room feel like it was already full before you got there.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: We should actually go tonight. Not make an appearance — actually go. Eat their food, talk about nothing, stay too late.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I just want to be clear about what kind of going. Not the kind where you stand near the food and clock everyone's stress indicators.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You did it just now. In the driveway. You mapped that entire group in about forty-five seconds.
Thirty seconds. But I wasn't going to correct her.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Marcus said Nadia mentioned the area. That she'd been the one to suggest the coast. He said it like he'd just remembered it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I work with you. Things leak. But Julian — people suggest places. She knew the coast, she mentioned it, he booked it. That's just how trips happen.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: He said it like he was remembering he'd found the listing. Not like he remembered someone else suggested it. There's a difference in how that sounds.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Memory is unreliable. You know this. You have built entire presentations around the fact that memory is unreliable.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Then file it and come eat something. And tonight — just be a person. Not an analyst. A person who is at a bonfire.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: I know you can. I've seen it happen.

blocks.prompt: Nadia mentioned the area. Marcus thinks the idea was his. Does it matter?

options.label: Not right now. File it. Be a person tonight.
Someone who suggests a specific location always has a reason for it. This stays open.
Mara's right. One detail doesn't make anything. But it doesn't disappear either.
